test_cases.question: You are tasked with performing radiographic testing (RT) inspection on a 16-inch diameter crude oil pipeline with 10 mm wall thickness. The pipeline operates at 700 psi and contains sour crude (H2S present). The girth welds require 100% examination per contract specifications. Which RT technique, source selection, and parameters will provide adequate radiographic sensitivity while meeting ASME V requirements and ensuring personnel safety?
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: For sour crude service pipeline radiography, the selection must balance image quality requirements with safety considerations for H2S service. Per ASME V Article 2 and API 1163 requirements: 1) Technique Selection: The double-wall double-image (DWDI) technique is preferred for 16-inch diameter pipe due to improved defect detectability in circumferential welds. Single-wall technique may be used if geometric unsharpness is controlled per ASME V T-274. 2) Source Selection: A high-activity Iridium-192 source (minimum 50 curies) is generally appropriate for 10mm wall thickness, providing adequate penetration with reasonable exposure times. Cobalt-60 should be avoided for 10mm wall as it produces excessive energy and reduced contrast. 3) Safety Considerations: Prior to exposure, verify H2S levels are below 10 ppm using personal gas monitors per 29 CFR 1910.1000. Establish 2 mSv/hr exposure boundary and use collimators to minimize scatter. For sour service, consider digital radiography (DR) to reduce retakes and personnel exposure. 4) Image Quality Requirements: Per ASME V T-283, ensure Image Quality Indicator (IQI) shows 2-1T sensitivity or better. Use wire-type or step-hole penetrameters on the source side. Required density range: 2.0-4.5 for film, 8-bit minimum (256 levels) for digital. 5) Geometric Considerations: Calculate Ug (geometric unsharpness) using Ug = d * t/D where d is source size, t is wall thickness, D is source-to-object distance. Ug must not exceed 0.5mm per ASME V T-274. Target SFD of 600mm minimum. 6) Acceptance Criteria: Apply ASME B31.3 or API 1104 acceptance criteria as specified. Porosity, lack of fusion, and crack-like indications must be evaluated against standard limits. Document all indications on radiographic report forms including location (clock position), length, and classification.

variants.abnormal_condition: Pipeline contains residual methanol from previous hydrotest
variants.modified_approach: Switch to phased array UT to eliminate ignition hazard
Reduce kV/mA settings for thinned sections

test_cases.question: For an automated ultrasonic testing (AUT) inspection of girth welds on a new 24-inch offshore natural gas pipeline (WT=18mm, X70 steel), describe the complete AUT procedure including zone discrimination setup, calibration requirements, scanning plan, and data analysis methodology per API 1163 and DNV-ST-F101.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Automated ultrasonic testing for offshore pipeline girth welds requires systematic zone discrimination approach per API 1163 Section 9 and DNV-ST-F101 Appendix C: 1) Zone Discrimination Setup: The weld is divided into zones based on wall thickness. For 18mm WT, typical zoning includes: Zone 1 (internal 0-6mm), Zone 2 (middle 6-12mm), Zone 3 (external 12-18mm). Each zone requires dedicated probe angles to optimize detection for expected defect orientations. Use TOFD channel for volumetric coverage and height measurement. 2) Probe Configuration: Deploy minimum 6 probes per side: 65° and 70° longitudinal shear wave probes for fusion face coverage, 45° for root inspection, 0° compression wave for lamination detection, plus TOFD pairs at 60° separation. Each probe covers approximately 1.5-2.0mm depth increment. Verify beam coverage using weld cross-section diagrams. Probe frequency: 5-10 MHz; offshore X70 typically uses 7-8 MHz. 3) Calibration Requirements: Per API 1163 Section 9.3, calibrate on reference block with 1.5mm side-drilled holes at fusion face, root, and cap positions. Reference reflector: 1.0mm diameter SDH for weld body, 3.0mm notch at fusion face. Calibration must include full DAC/TCG curve for each zone. Recalibration every 4 hours or when temperature changes >15°C. 4) Scanning Plan: Use encoder-driven scanner with minimum 1mm position resolution. Scan both sides. Maintain coupling pressure 20-40 psi. Scan speed maximum 150mm/s. Overlap coverage zones by minimum 10% at zone boundaries. 5) Data Analysis: Automated software identifies indications. Level 1: software auto-identification. Level 2: operator verification of all indications >40% DAC. Indications characterized by: amplitude, depth, length, and position. Use TOFD for accurate height measurement (±1mm accuracy). 6) Acceptance Criteria: Apply API 1104 Appendix A criteria: Planar defects >1.6mm height require evaluation.

variants.modified_approach: Use EDM-machined notches in sacrificial weld end
Switch to semi-automated with manual encoder wheel

test_cases.question: An MFL smart pig inspection was run on a 30-inch refined product pipeline (diesel, WT=9.5mm, API 5L Grade B). The inspection identified 247 metal loss anomalies. Detail the complete data analysis process per API 1163 and ASME B31.8.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: MFL data analysis per API 1163 Section 5: 1) Verify inspection completeness (>95% coverage). Check speed variations - MFL accuracy degrades above 5m/s. 2) Characterization: MFL provides depth estimates with ±10% accuracy for metal loss >20% WT. Size for depth (% WT), length (axial), width (circumferential), orientation. 3) Severity Classification per ASME B31.8 Section 841.2.4: Type 1-isolated pitting, Type 2-localized, Type 3-widespread. Thresholds: >40% WT evaluate, >50% repair, >80% immediate. 4) Interaction Criteria: Apply RSTRENG or Modified B31G for axial metal loss. Use API 579 Level 2 for circumferential. Interaction rule: Anomalies separated by <3× minimum remaining wall thickness are interacting. Combined length = sum plus separation. 5) Remaining Strength: Calculate burst pressure using Modified B31G or RSTRENG-1. Safety factor 0.72 applied to SMYS. If failure pressure <1.25×MAOP, excavation required. 6) Prioritization: Rank by immediate risk, corrosion growth rate, location class (HCAs first), accessibility. 7) Field Verification: UT or RT verification of MFL estimates. MFL depth accuracy ±10% WT.

variants.abnormal_condition: Corrosion circumferentially oriented at girth weld

test_cases.question: A geometry/caliper pig survey on a 36-inch natural gas pipeline (WT=12.7mm, API 5L X65) with 48 measurement fingers detected deformation anomalies. Describe data analysis per API 1163 and ASME B31.8.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Caliper pig data analysis per API 1163 Section 6: 1) Tool: 48-arm caliper provides radial measurements every ~2.356°. Verify measurement resolution (±0.1mm). Check calibration using weld beads (~1.5mm reference). 2) Classification: Dent (localized concave), Ovality (uniform cross-section), Buckle (compression failure), Ripple (multiple waves), Gouge (metal removal). 3) Dent Assessment per ASME B31.8 Section 841.2.3: depth, length, radius of curvature. Constrained dents more severe. Unconstrained: <6% OD acceptable at pipe body, <2% OD at weld. Calculate strain using ε = (2/3) × (depth²) / (length × radius). 4) Out-of-Roundness: Ovality = (Dmax - Dmin)/DNominal × 100%. <2% acceptable, 2-5% monitor, >5% evaluate. 5) Buckle: Local buckles >20% WT abrupt. Propagation buckles: check if P > Pp = 2π × E × (t/D)³ / (1-ν²). 6) Fatigue: Stress concentration >1.5 requires fatigue assessment. 7) Remediation: Immediate for constrained dents, dents >6% OD at body, dents at weld.

variants.abnormal_condition: Dent contains girth weld at apex
variants.modified_approach: Calculate total strain including weld reinforcement
Evaluate for upheaval or lateral buckling

test_cases.question: TOFD ultrasonic inspection on a 20-inch pipeline girth weld identified three crack-like indications in the HAZ with diffracted signals from upper and lower tips. Detail the TOFD analysis process per API 579.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: TOFD per API 579 Part 9: 1) Signal Interpretation: Validate by confirming UT and LT signals. Linear defects show curved TOFD patterns in B-scan. Verify >6dB SNR. Review A-scan for amplitude. 2) Locus Curve: The TOFD system uses locus curves representing constant time-of-flight. For each indication: Identify UT and LT positions in B-scan. Measure time-of-flight difference (Δt). Calculate depth: depth = V × Δt / 2, where V is shear wave velocity (typically 3.23 mm/μs for steel). 3) Height Sizing: TOFD height accuracy is ±1mm for cracks >2mm height. 4) Position: Measure crack length from longitudinal extent in merged scan. Map to clock position. 5) Characterization: Distinguish crack (planar), lack of fusion, slag inclusion, root concavity. For ECA, only planar defects are critical. 6) ECA Integration: Input dimensions into FAD per API 579 Part 9. Calculate stress intensity factor KI = Y × σ × √(πa). Calculate reference stress σref using limit load. Assessment point inside curve = acceptable. 7) Safety: Apply safety factor of 1.5 to crack height.

test_cases.question: A CIPS/DCVG survey on 75-mile, 24-inch pipeline identified 42 off-potentials more negative than -1.20V CSE and 18 DCVG drains >50mA. Describe analysis per NACE SP0169.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: CIPS/DCVG analysis per NACE SP0169: 1) CIPS Data: Off-potential -1.20V indicates GOOD protection (>-850mV required). Readings more negative than -1.20V suggest adequate polarization. 2) DCVG Analysis: Current drain >50mA indicates coating holidays. Calculate %IR = (ΔVon - ΔVoff) / ΔVon × 100%. Categories: <15% small holiday, 15-35% medium, 35-60% large, >60% severe. Prioritize >35% for excavation. 3) Severity Ranking: Immediate repair: DCVG >60%. Monitor: DCVG 35-60%. Deferred: DCVG <35%. 4) IR Drop Considerations: On-potential includes IR drop from anode to cathode. Off-potential at 0.2-0.5s post-interruption eliminates most IR error. If on-potential >-2.0V but off-potential >-850mV, system adequate. 5) AC Corrosion Risk: AC voltage >4V (on disconnected pipe) indicates AC corrosion risk. AC density (mA/m²) = AC Voltage / (coating resistivity × area). High-risk >100 A/m². Mitigate with sacrificial anodes or polarization cells. 6) Coating Assessment: Small holidays acceptable if CP maintained. Large holidays (>25cm²) require repair.

variants.abnormal_condition: Stray current from DC traction system

test_cases.question: Two MFL inspections on 16-inch wet gas pipeline 3 years apart: First 85 anomalies @ 15% WT, Second 112 @ 22% WT with 68 original showing growth. Detail analysis per NACE SP0208.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Corrosion rate analysis per NACE SP0208: 1) Anomaly Matching: Match between runs using odometer (±1%), clock position (±15°). Of 85 original, 68 showed growth (80% active), 17 no change, 19 new anomalies. 2) Growth Rate: CR = (Depth₂ - Depth₁) / (Time₂ - Time₁). Average = (22% - 15%) / 3 = 2.33% WT/year. 3) Statistical Analysis: Calculate mean (2.33%/year), standard deviation, 90th percentile growth rate. Use 90th percentile for conservative assessment. 4) Remaining Life: RL = (Failure_Depth - Current_Depth) / Growth_Rate. Using 80% WT failure: RL = (80% - 22%) / 2.33% = 24.9 years. Using 90th percentile (4%/year): RL = (80-22)/4 = 14.5 years. 5) Re-inspection Interval: Per API 1163 Section 12.8, interval = RL / Safety Factor. Class 1: 14.5/2 = 7 years. HCA: 14.5/4 = 3-4 years. 6) Uncertainty: ILI uncertainty ±10% WT. 7) Mitigation: Inhibitors reduce rates 50-90%.

test_cases.question: Holiday detection on 42-inch pipeline with 3LPE coating. Spark tester at 15kV identified 127 holidays including pinholes, voids, thin areas. Detail per CSA Z245.21.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Holiday detection per CSA Z245.21: 1) Detection Voltage: V = 125 × √(thickness in μm). For 3mm (3000μm): V = 125 × √3000 = 6.85kV. 15kV is TOO HIGH and may damage good coating - maximum should be ~10kV. 2) Defect Classification: Pinhole (>0.5mm diameter, penetration to steel), Void (incomplete filling), Thin area (<70% specified thickness), Scorched (carbonized), Wrinkle (lifted). 3) Mapping: Mark with paint pen. GPS coordinates. Categorize: Small (<3mm), Medium (3-10mm), Large (>10mm). Map concentration areas. 4) Repair: Clean with solvent, feather edges 50mm. Pinholes <10mm: epoxy filler putty. Areas >25mm: repair patch with heat-shrink sleeve. Confirm repair with holiday test at 80% voltage. Document repair with photo. 5) Acceptance: <1 holiday per 10m² for pinholes. <1 thin area per 5m². Field repairs: zero holidays. Holiday rate >5 per 10m² requires investigation.

variants.modified_approach: Verify at weld cap; repair with epoxy

test_cases.question: New ICCP system with 4 deep anode beds (32 anodes each, 2.5A/anode) and 6 rectifiers for 120-mile, 30-inch pipeline. Describe commissioning per NACE SP0169.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: ICCP commissioning per NACE SP0169: 1) Pre-Checks: Verify rectifier installation, AC power, DC terminals, shunt meters. Anode bed resistance <2 ohms. Test stations at 1-mile rural, 0.5-mile urban. 2) Current Requirement: Apply temporary DC current, measure >300mV shift. For 30-inch × 120-mile: Surface area = π × 0.762m × 193km = 462,000 m². Current density 1-5 mA/m². Total requirement 460-2300A. 3) Energizing: Start at lowest tap. Record DC voltage, DC current. Calculate R = V/I per bed. 4) IR-Free Potential: After 24-hour polarization, synchronous interruption. Instant-off potential >-850mV CSE required. -950mV if SRB present. Maximum -3.0V to avoid hydrogen embrittlement. 5) Profile Analysis: Minimum -850mV at midpoint between rectifiers. Increase output if >-0.80V observed. 6) AC Voltage: >15V indicates AC corrosion risk. Install grounding cells. 7) Acceptance: 95% of test stations meet -850mV, current within 20% design, no over-protection.

test_cases.question: Plan pre-service hydrostatic test on 15-mile, 20-inch crude oil pipeline (X65, WT=12mm). Test pressure 1.25×MAOP (1500 psi). Detail safety per ASME B31.4.
test_cases.expected_response_detailed: Hydrotest safety per ASME B31.4: 1) Stored Energy: Volume = π × r² × L = π × (0.254m)² × 24140m = 4,890 m³. For water at 1500 psi, energy = P × V × 0.5 = 25.3 MJ (~6 kg TNT equivalent). 2) Exclusion Zone: Minimum = 0.5 × √(P × D²) = 0.5 × √(1500 × 400) = 388 ft (~118m). Conservative: 300m. Mark with barriers every 50m. 3) Personnel: Non-essential evacuated. Operators at control manifold, minimum 100m. Radio communication. 4) Ramp Rate: Maximum 100 psi per hour. Stage holds: 50% (750 psi) 30 min, 90% (1350 psi) 30 min. Final to 1500 psi at 25 psi/hr. PRV at 105% test pressure. 5) Monitoring: Visual every 15 min, acoustic listening, digital pressure logger, temperature recording. 6) Emergency: Pressure drop >5% in 1 hour - stop test. Rupture - automatic depressure, evacuation alarm. 7) Acceptance: No visible leaks, <1% pressure drop over 8 hours, repairs 100% RT/UT prior to retest.
